title: citrus × aurantium var. japonica
intro: Citrus × aurantium var. japonica, commonly known as bitter orange, is a variety of the Seville orange tree (Citrus aurantium) that is native to East Asia. Bitter orange has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, improve circulation, and relieve muscle spasms. The health benefits of bitter orange include its potential to aid in weight loss, improve mood, and reduce anxiety due to its high levels of flavonoids and limonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bitter orange is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and stress relief.
